auth_notes: Requires Login with Amazon (LWA) for access tokens and AWS IAM credentials for signing requests. Every request must be signed using the AWS Signature Version 4 algorithm, which is difficult to implement in standard HTTP nodes.
rate_limits: Uses a 'Leaky Bucket' algorithm. Rates vary by endpoint; for example, GetOrders has a burst of 20 and a sustain rate of 1 request per second.
gotchas: AWS SigV4 signing requires a Code node to manually calculate HMAC signatures for headers.
The SP-API sandbox environment is limited and often returns static mock responses rather than processing actual logic.
Strict rate limits frequently lead to 429 errors if not handled with precise wait/retry logic.
research_notes: Implementation requires a 'Code' node to handle the AWS SigV4 signing process using the 'crypto' library. This node will generate the 'Authorization' and 'X-Amz-Date' headers required by the subsequent HTTP Request node. Users must also register as a developer in Seller Central to obtain the LWA credentials.

This node will generate the 'Authorization' and 'X-Amz-Date' headers required by the subsequent HTTP Request node.
